items.title: Apple’s weird anti-nausea dots cured my car sickness
items.description: I'll just work from the car, I thought. But after a few minutes of staring at my screen on quick mountain switchbacks I could feel the first signs of cold, coagulated nausea bubbling up from that sweaty place in my gut. I looked to the horizon for relief, but nothing helped… until I remembered Apple...

items.title: SpaceX is officially buying Cursor for $60 billion
items.description: Days after its massive IPO, SpaceX says it is spending $60 billion to buy Cursor - a bet designed to help Elon Musk's sprawling rocket / AI / social media behemoth win over lucrative enterprise customers and close the gap with AI rivals like Anthropic and OpenAI. The takeover was not entirely unexpe...

items.title: Schlage’s UWB-enabled smart lock launches this month
items.description: It's been more than a year since Schlage announced its first smart lock to support ultra wideband technology (UWB), but now it's finally almost available to purchase. Starting June 29th, the Schlage Sense Pro deadbolt lock will be available for $399 in the US, allowing customers to unlock their door...

items.title: These mechanical keyboards are two very different sides of the same beautifully made coin
items.description: Most mechanical keyboards are great these days, with colorful looks and satisfying typing sounds - even in budget-friendly ranges. But every so often, one stops me dead in my tracks. In this case, two of them. I've been testing a pair of jaw-droppingly lovely keyboards that launched late last year. ...

items.title: The Microsoft Surface Laptop 8 and Surface Pro 12 now come with Snapdragon X2 chips
items.description: Microsoft is launching new Surface Laptops and a Surface Pro with Qualcomm Snapdragon X2 processors. items.title: Half a billion people are using Threads every month
items.description: Threads has surpassed 500 million monthly active users, Meta announced on Tuesday, hitting the milestone just shy of the platform's third birthday. items.title: Kodak’s collectible Charmera camera is getting new Y2K-inspired designs
items.description: Despite being an objectively terrible digital camera, the Kodak Charmera has been incredibly popular thanks to a cheap price tag and several fun retro designs inspired by the iconic 1987 single-use Kodak Fling.
